i suggest to add the type of issues fixed in the commit message like below..

Fix Blocker/Critical sonar issues

Fix Blocker/Critical sonar issues in ccsdk/sli/core module
https://sonar.onap.org/component_issues?id=org.onap.ccsdk.sli.core%3Accsdk-sli-core#resolved=false|severities=MAJOR
*The type of the "arguments" object should be an interface such as "List" rather than the implementation "ArrayList".
*Either log or rethrow exception.

Issue-Id: CCSDK-67
Change-Id: I3d5f92c3c93a967a5eb368988dfef1be1af047ee
Signed-off-by: shashikanth.vh <shashikanth.vh@huawei.com>

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

I agree with Ramu's comment - I'm not going to block, but in the future if you could please list the types of issues being addressed and especially summarize them in the summary, it would be helpful.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

There is a reason why ArrayList has been used

Patch Set 2:

I see you added logging to exceptions when closing Closables.
This is unnecessary and polutes code.

Patch Set 2:

Thanks Rich for the review, I'll revert this change, may I know the reason for using ArrayList instead of interface ?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

I'm hesitant to approve this since Rich said ArrayList is needed. Rich - can you please explain further?

Abandoned

Has been inactive for too long and can no longer be merged

Patch Set 2:

Hi Shashikanth,
Even though using List<>, as you have modified is the right way to pass the data to the method, in this design I prefer that call is done using the array. I could have done it by using an Object[], but that would not be elegant. Therefore I used ArrayList. When working with prepared statement it is more convenient in my opinion to iterate over the array, as opposed to iterating over a list.
I am sorry that your change got rejected, but we had a conflict between this 1 file change and my feature change I was committing.
I apologize for rejecting your change, but gerrit is not handle conflicts well, and was leading to a delay in committing changes.
I did try to import your changes manually.

Patch Set 2:

Shashikanth, btw, thank you for your contributions to the code.
